title: Hierarchy of Formal Languages and Automata
description: A visual representation of the Chomsky hierarchy, showing the relationship between classes of formal languages, the automata that recognize them, and the grammars that generate them, as covered in Hopcroft's foundational work.
relationships: Linear Bounded Automata recognize Context-Sensitive Languages
Turing Machines recognize Recursively Enumerable Languages
Each class is a proper subset of the next (e.g., Regular ⊂ Context-Free ⊂ Context-Sensitive ⊂ Recursively Enumerable)
